Falls Are a Silent Crisis

Every year, millions of older adults experience falls that lead to serious injuries, hospitalization, and loss of independence. The emotional and financial toll on families is staggering.

684,000

Fall-related hospitalizations annually in the U.S.

37.3M

Adults 65+ who fall each year worldwide

70%

Of falls happen at home, where seniors feel safest

Prevention, Not Reaction

We believe technology should protect the people you love without changing how they live. SageStep combines medical-grade sensors with everyday comfort to create the world's first predictive fall-prevention system worn right on your feet.

The Product

Meet SageStep

Discreet, comfortable, and machine-washable smart socks woven with proprietary pressure-mapping fabric. Our advanced algorithm learns each user's unique gait pattern and alerts caregivers to subtle changes that predict falls before they happen.

Machine Washable
24/7 Comfort
How It Works

Three Simple Steps to Safety

1

Wear Daily

Put on SageStep like any other pair of socks. The sensors begin learning your natural walking pattern within hours.

2

Monitor Gait

Our AI continuously analyzes balance, pressure distribution, and stride changes in real time.

3

Get Alerts

Caregivers receive gentle notifications through the app when subtle changes suggest increased fall risk.

Technology

Technology & Features

4-Signal Fusion

EDA, pulse rate, skin temperature, and AI-powered gait analysis combined for comprehensive health assessment.

Zero Charging Required

Swappable rechargeable battery system enables continuous monitoring without daily charging interruptions. Machine washable for everyday convenience.

Family App

Real-time health overview, risk indicators, emergency alerts, and AI voice reminders — all in one connected platform.

Nursing Home Dashboard

Two-way sync between family and facility, enabling coordinated care for multiple residents simultaneously.

The App

Your Care Command Center

The SageStep app transforms raw health data into actionable insights. See the full picture of your loved one's wellbeing, wherever you are.

Live Health Timeline

Scroll through a visual timeline of daily activity, sleep quality, and mobility trends.

Smart Alert Triage

Distinguish between routine updates, early warnings, and urgent alerts with color-coded urgency levels.

AI Voice Companion

A gentle voice assistant reminds seniors to hydrate, take medication, or go for a walk when inactivity is detected.

Circle of Care

Invite family members, nurses, and doctors to view shared dashboards with customizable permission levels.

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

How does SageStep predict falls?

Our fabric-embedded sensors continuously measure pressure distribution, gait symmetry, and balance stability. Our AI compares these patterns against your personal baseline and detects subtle degradations that typically precede falls by days or weeks.

Is it comfortable for all-day wear?

Absolutely. SageStep is made from a premium bamboo-cotton blend with a seamless toe and cushioned sole. Most users forget they're wearing smart socks after the first hour.

Do I need a smartphone to use it?

The socks pair with a small hub device that connects to WiFi. Family members and caregivers use the mobile app to receive alerts and view health trends, but the senior wearer doesn't need to interact with technology at all.

How often do the socks need to be replaced?

Each pair lasts approximately 6 months with regular washing and daily wear. The app will notify you when it's time to refresh your pair to ensure sensor accuracy.

Is my health data secure?

Yes. All data is encrypted end-to-end using AES-256 and HIPAA-compliant cloud storage. You control exactly who can view your gait insights.

Voices from Evergreen Care Home, Shanghai

What Seniors & Caregivers Are Saying

"I am 92 and still manage my daily life on my own here. I eat on time, take my walks, and video-call my children every evening. If this smart sock can let my family know I am doing well without me having to do anything, I would be happy to wear it — but it must be soft and comfortable."

— 秦爷爷, Resident, Evergreen Care Home

"The activities here keep me busy, and my family checks on me every day. I chose to stay because I feel safe. A device that reminds me to take my medicine, drink water, or get up and move would be very helpful — as long as it is simple and does not confuse me."

— 陈奶奶, Resident, Evergreen Care Home

"Night-time falls are our biggest worry. Residents who are still independent prefer to get up on their own, so we only know something happened after the fact. If we could catch balance changes or detect a fall instantly, it would change everything for our residents and their families."

— Nursing Staff, Evergreen Care Home
